title: Fixing Type Inference for a Custom Class Property

statement.markdown:
Write a TypeScript function to parse a custom class object and return the property value of `address`.

The class object will have the following structure:

```json
{ "name": "John Doe", "age": 30, "address": { "street": "123 Main St", "city": "Anytown", "state": "CA", "zip": "12345" } }
```
statement.inputFormat: A JSON string representing the custom class object
statement.outputFormat: The value of the `address` property as a string
statement.notes: Note that the `address` property is an object itself, and you should return its string representation.

constraints.description: Maximum depth of the address object

examples.explanation: Return the address property as a string

hints.text: Use a TypeScript function to parse the JSON string

editorial.title: Editorial Title
editorial.intuition: Use a recursive function to parse the address object
commonMistakes: Not parsing the address object recursively
